reason: Both correctly land on "fix, don't kill" with accurate LTV:CAC (2.2 vs 3:1 benchmark) and payback (~6 months) reasoning, and both give concrete waste-cutting fixes and kill triggers. B covers more of the specified fix mechanisms — explicit intent-tier segmentation (bottom-funnel/competitor terms vs. category terms) and landing page/offer match testing — plus a sharp churn insight (13-month implied retention). A's distinguishing strength is mentioning cheaper compounding channels (SEO/outbound) as a comparison point, which B omits. Neither nails blended-vs-paid CAC or branded-vs-generic split explicitly. On balance B covers slightly more of the key decision points without any loss of correctness or added padding, giving it a narrow edge.
